claims.text: UNHCR's Operational Data Portal for the Sudan situation records Total Forcibly Displaced at 11,431,220 (last updated 06 July 2026), comprising 6,597,438 IDPs inside Sudan (last updated 15 June 2026), 273,081 self-relocated refugees in Sudan (last updated 06 July 2026), and 4,560,701 total newly arrived refugees, asylum-seekers and returnees outside Sudan (last updated 06 July 2026).
UNHCR describes the Sudan situation as the world's largest displacement, humanitarian and protection crisis.

description: The conflict that erupted in Sudan in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces has produced what UNHCR calls the world's largest displacement crisis, forcing millions from their homes inside Sudan and into neighbouring countries.

impact.summary: As of UNHCR's Operational Data Portal update on 06 July 2026, Total Forcibly Displaced in the Sudan situation stood at 11,431,220 — 6,597,438 IDPs inside Sudan, 273,081 self-relocated refugees within Sudan, and 4,560,701 newly arrived refugees, asylum-seekers and returnees outside the country. UNHCR continues to describe Sudan as the world's largest displacement, humanitarian and protection crisis.

name: Sudan Displacement Crisis
